encounter_context.encounter_reason: 62-year-old man with hypertension and prior CAD presenting with 2 hours of substernal chest pain; serial troponins rising and ECG shows new inferior ST-elevation.

chronic_conditions: Coronary artery disease with prior PCI
recent_changes: Troponin rose from 0.05 to 4.2 over two hours.
New inferior ST-elevation on repeat ECG.

medications.name: Aspirin 325 mg
medications.why_important: Loading dose required before PCI; do not withhold.

medications.name: Ticagrelor 180 mg load
medications.why_important: Dual antiplatelet load before PCI; must not be confused with clopidogrel.

medications.name: Heparin drip
medications.why_important: Anticoagulation for STEMI during and after PCI.

safety_critical_facts: STEMI diagnosis confirmed — do not delay cath lab transfer.
Dual antiplatelet plus heparin has been loaded; hand off these doses explicitly.
Patient has an ASA allergy history that was ruled out before loading; document clearly.
disposition_and_follow_up: Transfer to cardiac cath lab; admit to CCU post-procedure.

supporting_source_excerpts.label: ECG report
supporting_source_excerpts.text: New 2 mm inferior ST-elevation with reciprocal depression.
